pages.full_text:
enthusiastically back towards Dinas.
	Afterwards we continued the walk + Eve got cross + said she wasn't enjoying it – scrambling uphill on all 4s. 500 ft in one mile. So we abandon the plan to reach the tops of Rhinog + just walk through the saddle between the hills. Perhaps more walking will have to wait until we all have boots rather than open sandals.
	We found shepherds herding sheep on our way down, + ~~[?derle]~~ deserted farm buildings. Simon, Thomas + Peter frolicked in the river when we reached the valley bottom, while Eve lay collapsed on the turf.
	We had another barbque that evening
	At 9.30. the wind picked up + Simon wondered whether the tent would hold, because ropes had snapped at Thorpeness last summer.
	After hours of worrying, Eve brought John into the van to join Charles + Peter, while
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence, continuing from A_0058. The word before 'deserted' is struck through — difficult to read; possibly 'derle' or 'dodo'; transcribed as ~~[?derle]~~ to indicate strikethrough. 'barbque' is the diary's spelling (abbreviation for barbecue). 'Rhinog' refers to the Rhinog/Rhinogydd hills in Gwynedd, Wales. Year context from adjacent spreads confirms this is May 1992 (May 23rd Sat confirmed as 1992 in A_0057; this is continuing the same trip leading to Wed 27th May on the right page). Text continues onto right page.

pages.full_text:
Simon tested the pegs. Then half an hour later at 2.30. we brought in Thomas + William while Simon collapsed the tent. Laura's dome spider tent was fine. John + Charles seemed to take those manoeuvres in their stride.
	We were all tired the next morning. Nevertheless

__Wed 27th May__
	We set off for Harlech + spent the morning there going up + down the tower before setting off for home. We had lunch at Trawsfynydd, a damned lake. Thomas had bought a frisbee + Peter, glasses.
	Then we stopped at Llanfair for Simon + [?+Laura] the boys to go on the train. It left at 4.15. We happened to meet up with the Bradishes. Eve met them at
pages.notes: Page number 115 visible top-right. Text at top continues from left page (page 114). 'Wed 27th May' is underlined and functions as a date heading. Year 1992 inferred: in A_0057 (pages 110–111) 'May 23rd Sat' and 'Sun 24th May' appear in the same Wales trip sequence; May 23 fell on a Saturday in 1992, making May 27 a Wednesday — consistent. 'Harlech' is underlined. After 'Simon +' there appears to be a small superscript annotation, possibly '+Laura' — transcribed as [?+Laura]. 'Llanfair' likely refers to Llanfair Caereinion, terminus of the Welshpool & Llanfair Light Railway (narrow-gauge steam railway). 'Bradishes' is a family surname — spelling uncertain, could be 'Bradshaws' or similar but 'Bradishes' is what appears written. 'a damned lake' — Trawsfynydd is a reservoir/lake in Gwynedd associated with a nuclear power station; the diarist's comment likely reflects its appearance. Text ends mid-sentence at bottom of page.
